name: Nus
description: A quick-fingered Purewater street thief and loyal friend of Randall -- lookout, fence, and second pair of hands for a job; nimble, streetwise, and easy to underestimate.
narrative: Nus runs the same canals and rooftops as Randall and counts him a true friend -- the pair grew up working the alleys of Purewater together. Wiry and quick, with a fence's eye for value and a lookout's nose for trouble, Nus is the one Randall calls when a job needs a second set of hands or a fast way out. Loyal to his mates and slippery with everyone else.

equipment.name: Leather jerkin (all locations)
equipment.notes: AP 1 each location; no armor penalty

extras.class_note: Competent Rank-1 street thief; a notch below Randall (Sleight 65, Lockpicking 62, Stealth 68, combat 65). Lookout / fence / partner-in-crime.
extras.role: Purewater street thief; friend and occasional partner of Randall.
extras.relationship: Friend of Randall (one of his named associates -- 'Nus, Asphodel, Mariarta' in Randall's notes); grew up working the Purewater alleys together.
abilities: Climb Walls (ignore 1 grade of Athletics difficulty to climb)
Hide in Shadows (Perception to spot is 1 grade harder)
extras.authored_note: New NPC -- no Roll20 sheet existed. Entirely GM-authored from the brief ('a thief called Nus, friend of Randall') and Randall's notes; tune freely.
extras.source: Authored for the And then the Dragons Came campaign (friend of Randall)
